methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Atom Computing is a Series B semiconductor company founded by Ben Bloom and Jonathan King, having raised $60M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 8 private and 3 public semiconductor firms, including stage-matched peers such as Positron AI, Etched, and d-Matrix.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement to the $60M total raised proxy, implying a valuation of $114M. Peer-median anchoring references 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies with valuations ranging from $500M to $5.0B; the median across this set is $1.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied—while the 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) trade at a median 22.3x price-to-sales, Atom Computing's revenue is not disclosed, precluding P/S multiple application.

The triangulated analysis yields a low of $97M, median of $557M, and high of $1.1B. The wide range reflects material tension between the stage-progression method, which anchors to capital deployed, and the peer-median approach, which reflects current market sentiment for frontier semiconductor ventures. Key uncertainties include Atom's revenue generation trajectory, differentiation relative to better-capitalized peers, and the sustainability of current private valuations in this sector.
